commit.message:
fix(seo): remove [slug]/loading.tsx that hides H1 from crawlers (#830)

The loading.tsx at the [slug] level created an automatic Suspense
boundary around ALL storefront sub-pages. During SSR, Next.js rendered
StorefrontPageSkeleton as the initial HTML (no H1), with actual page
content only in RSC streaming payload scripts. Crawlers that don't
execute JavaScript (Ahrefs, some Googlebot modes) only saw the
skeleton — zero H1 tags.

The home page already has its own <Suspense> wrapper with the skeleton
fallback, so this file was redundant there and harmful for SEO on all
other pages (about, privacy, faq, contact, terms, etc.).

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
ci(tests): keep backend diagnostics while raising timeout

Summary:
- keep the backend test step at pytest -v for full CI diagnostics
- retain the higher 10 minute timeout headroom for the expanded backend suite

Rationale:
- the local full backend suite completes cleanly, so the PR failure was timeout pressure rather than a reproduced hang
- verbose output is still useful when CI fails, so the timeout fix should not trade away diagnostics

Tests:
- cd backend && OPENROUTER_API_KEY=test-key WORKSPACE_DIR=/tmp/seraph-test UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest -q
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
ci(tests): raise backend timeout headroom

Summary:
- increase the backend test job timeout from 5 to 10 minutes
- switch the backend pytest invocation from -v to -q to reduce log overhead

- the failing check was a job timeout, not a reproducible hang in the extensions slice

commit.message:
fix(web): honor DAO RPC ordering in wagmi config (#560)

Summary:
- normalize DAO chain metadata in a shared helper reused by the dapp
  provider
- build explicit viem transports from DAO-configured RPC URLs, keep
  their fallback order stable, and fail fast when none are configured
- avoid adding the built-in mainnet chain when the DAO itself already
  runs on chain id 1

Rationale:
- duplicate mainnet entries let RainbowKit and wagmi prefer built-in
  Ethereum RPC defaults over daoConfig.chain.rpcs
- explicit fallback transports preserve DAO RPC priority and fail over
  to the next configured endpoint when one RPC is unavailable
- empty RPC lists should fail with a clear DAO-chain error instead of
  deferring to an unusable transport with no URL

Tests:
- pnpm exec eslint src/config/wagmi.ts src/providers/dapp.provider.tsx
  src/components/members-table/hooks/useMembersData.ts
- pnpm exec tsc --noEmit
- pnpm dlx tsx -e ordered fallback transport probe
- pnpm dlx tsx -e empty RPC error probe
- curl -fsSL https://api.degov.ai/dao/config/fluence-dao?format=yml

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(capabilities): preserve extension approval boundaries

Summary:
- route extension-backed skill and workflow repairs to manual enable_extension actions instead of direct toggle actions
- keep bootstrap from auto-applying non-safe extension enable actions and dedupe repeated package enable recommendations
- add backend and cockpit regressions covering manual extension-enable repairs and approval-required lifecycle responses

Rationale:
- packaged capabilities must go through the extension lifecycle approval path before they can be re-enabled
- bootstrap should not mark approval-gated repairs as applied or hide them from the operator

fix: handle transient L1 RPC failures (#22)

## Summary
- add explicit retry helpers around L1 head and block-input RPC reads
- keep the continuous block stream alive on repeated L1 failures and
skip the block after retries instead of bubbling the error up and
shutting down the runtime
- reuse the same retry wrapper for single-block RPC fetches and make
EthProofs upload logs only report success on actual success
- checked the local Alloy 1.4.3 retry layer sources; the built-in policy
is centered on retryable transport/rate-limit responses, so
connection-timeout handling stays explicit at the block-stream boundary

## Testing
- `ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=1 cargo check -p ethereum_prover --lib`
- `RUST_MIN_STACK=16777216 ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=1 cargo test -p
ethereum_prover --lib -- --nocapture`
- `RUST_MIN_STACK=16777216 ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=1 cargo nextest run -p
ethereum_prover --lib`

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(web): move LLM markdown routes to /api/llm/ to fix storefront routing (#827)

* fix(web): move LLM markdown routes to /api/llm/ to fix storefront routing collision

The `.md` directory routes (e.g., `index.html.md/route.ts`) inside the
storefront `[slug]/` route tree caused a Next.js App Router collision
with the dynamic `[category]` segment. On custom domains like
ogabassey.com, the homepage resolved to a category page titled
"Index.Html.Md" instead of the merchant storefront.

Fix: Consolidate all 8 per-page `.md` route handlers into a single
catch-all API route at `/api/llm/[...segments]/route.ts`. The proxy
now intercepts `.md` requests in three contexts (custom domains,
subdomains, and slug-based paths) and rewrites them to the API handler.

The external URLs remain spec-compliant (e.g., /index.html.md still
returns markdown), but the routes no longer live in the storefront
tree where they can collide with dynamic segments.

Introduced in: c9cb732d6 (PR #785)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add colocated tests for consolidated /api/llm route (#827)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address CodeRabbit review findings in PR #827

- Convert NOT_FOUND constant to notFound() factory to avoid Response body
  reuse (a Response body can only be consumed once)
- Use host header for origin derivation so custom domains resolve correctly
  on Vercel instead of returning internal deployment hostname

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: restore multicall batching for DAO RPC reads (#559)

* fix(web): restore multicall batching for RPC reads

Summary:
- fix the shared multicall batch size to use viem's byte-based default
- configure wagmi transports from each chain's DAO RPC list via fallback
- make shared wagmi multicall batching explicit for public read traffic

Rationale:
- the previous batch size of 10 bytes split existing multicall reads into
  near-single-call requests
- RainbowKit's default transport setup only created one http transport per
  chain, which skipped the DAO RPC fallback list from config
- reducing fan-out at the shared client layer lowers pressure on public RPCs
  without rewriting each page-level hook

Tests:
- cd packages/web && pnpm lint
- cd packages/web && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
(cherry picked from commit cb724fb8759aa7ef9421e4e117be0313dfc6ee8c)

* fix(web): harden wagmi transport selection

Summary:
- preserve DAO RPC ordering by disabling viem fallback ranking
- dedupe configured chains and transports by chain id before creating wagmi
  config
- fail fast when a chain has no configured RPC URLs

Rationale:
- automated review surfaced edge cases that could silently reorder RPC
  selection or duplicate mainnet entries
- raising an explicit config error is easier to diagnose than a later
  request-time transport failure

Tests:
- cd packages/web && pnpm lint
- cd packages/web && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

## Summary

airbender build` is run without `--project`

with focused unit tests

commit.message:
Fix staging deploy after core dependency sync

Allow the Cloudflare Pages deploy workflow to run via workflow_dispatch so dependency sync updates can deploy the branch they modify.

Grant the sync workflow permission to dispatch actions and trigger deploy.yml explicitly after it pushes a dependency bump, avoiding GitHub's suppression of follow-up workflow runs from GITHUB_TOKEN pushes.

Document the deployment behavior so the setup notes match the actual trigger path used by automated sync updates.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Fix presence subscription update loops

Stabilize presence observers across PresenceUsers, BringToMe, and UserTracking to prevent repeated subscriptions from driving React into a maximum update depth error.

Use a stable presence subject per effect, initialize state from the current presence snapshot when the effect mounts, and unsubscribe from the same captured subject during cleanup.

In PresenceUsers, guard user, follower, and tracked-user state updates so unchanged presence payloads do not trigger redundant rerenders.

Verification: bun run build completed the production build step, but the repository still has pre-existing TypeScript errors in unrelated areas during the type-generation phase.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(symphony): add rolling TPS sparkline to TUI dashboard (#147)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(seo): resolve sitemap 500 — read slug from headers, not params (#826)

* fix(seo): read merchant slug from headers instead of params in sitemap

Next.js 16 with generateSitemaps() only passes { id: Promise<string> }
to the sitemap handler — it does NOT forward route params. The function
was crashing with "TypeError: Cannot destructure" on props.params.

Fix: read merchant slug from proxy-set headers (x-merchant-slug for
subdomains, x-custom-domain for custom domains, host fallback).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(domain-cache): only log error on actual Supabase errors, not missing domains

Missing domain lookups (no data, no error) are normal — return null silently
instead of logging a misleading error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Implement hierarchy navigation UI for groups and frames

Add explicit parent navigation to the floating context toolbar, render hierarchy breadcrumbs above the selection UI, and upgrade the side-panel items view into a nested hierarchy tree. Tree nodes now support direct selection, expansion state, and distinct visual treatment for frames versus groups while keeping canvas selection direct and item-first.

Reuse the core selection hierarchy APIs for parent promotion and ancestor navigation instead of duplicating hierarchy state in the UI. Wire the feature into the existing hotkey path with a Shift+Enter parent-selection shortcut, add focused tests for hierarchy helpers and hotkey definition, and update localized labels for the new controls.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(seo): ensure H1 tags render in SSR HTML for storefront pages (#825)

* fix(seo): ensure H1 tags render in SSR HTML for storefront pages

Ahrefs crawl detected 0 H1 tags on 8 storefront pages because
StorefrontPageWrapper loaded templates via client-side useEffect,
causing SSR to render a skeleton with no H1.

Fix: resolve template components server-side (matching the blog page
pattern) so H1 tags appear in the initial HTML for crawlers.

- about, privacy, faq, contact, terms: replace StorefrontPageWrapper
  with server-side template resolution via getTemplate().getComponents()
- account, wishlist: extract client component, add server-rendered H1
- wallet: add sr-only H1 in server component wrapper

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address CodeRabbit review findings in PR #825

- Fix CRITICAL: wishlist API calls now use auth cookies instead of
  broken email-based lookup that returned 401 for guests
- Fix CRITICAL: remove fetchWishList from useEffect dependency array
  to prevent infinite refetch loop; inline async logic in effect
- Fix duplicate H1 tags on wallet and wishlist pages by removing
  sr-only server H1 where client component already renders one
- Move merchant country fetch to server component (page.tsx) and
  pass as prop, eliminating client-side data waterfall
- Add try/catch to handleLogout in account-client to prevent
  broken state on sign-out failure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(seo): resolve Google rich results validation errors on storefront pages (#824)

* fix(db): resolve RLS advisor warnings and platform admin login redirect

Platform admin merchant was invisible to its owner due to a blanket RLS
SELECT policy filtering out is_platform_admin rows. Split into role-specific
policies (anon/authenticated) so owners always see their own merchant.

Also fixes all Supabase advisor warnings:
- Security: platform_revenue view security_invoker, function search_path,
  materialized views (platform_daily_summary, top_merchants, merchant_health)
  revoked from public API and wrapped in admin-only RPCs

commit.message:
feat(extensions): enforce package permissions and approvals

Summary:
- add manifest permission evaluation for packaged skills, workflows, and connectors, and surface the resulting approval profile through the extension lifecycle APIs
- keep permission-invalid packaged skills and workflows out of the active runtime, and block degraded packages from being re-enabled until validation issues are fixed
- require lifecycle approval for high-risk installs/enables using fingerprints bound to the validated package path and content digest, with regression coverage and doc updates

Rationale:
- make extension permissions enforceable at install and enable time instead of treating manifests as advisory metadata
- prevent approval replay against changed local packages and keep degraded packages from consuming operator approvals they cannot satisfy
- document the real lifecycle behavior so package authors know when validation failures hard-block install versus leave installed packages repairable

Tests:
- PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/pycache python3 -m py_compile backend/src/api/extensions.py backend/src/extensions/lifecycle.py backend/src/extensions/permissions.py backend/src/extensions/registry.py backend/src/extensions/doctor.py backend/src/skills/manager.py backend/src/workflows/manager.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_extension_doctor.py tests/test_extensions_api.py tests/test_skills.py tests/test_workflows.py
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check
- reviewer subagent: no findings after re-review

* **New Features**

failures and abort.

human-readable summaries.

bindings, strings, hello world) and failing suites (compile errors,
runtime assertion failures) with expected output fixtures.

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Refine vehicle fuel-first mobile layout

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(extensions): bridge manifest-backed MCP connectors

Summary:
- add typed MCP connector parsing, doctor validation, and registry metadata for manifest-backed connector packs
- sync packaged MCP servers into lifecycle install/enable/disable/remove flows and route catalog MCP installs through generated extension packages
- cover the bridge with registry, doctor, lifecycle, and catalog tests, including stable validation errors and real catalog-to-runtime registration

Rationale:
- make MCP a first-class connector contribution inside the extension platform instead of a parallel runtime-only path
- keep catalog installs and lifecycle controls consistent with the new package model while failing predictably on invalid connector definitions

Tests:
- PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/pycache python3 -m py_compile backend/src/extensions/connectors.py backend/src/extensions/registry.py backend/src/extensions/lifecycle.py backend/src/extensions/doctor.py backend/src/api/catalog.py backend/tests/test_extension_registry.py backend/tests/test_extension_doctor.py backend/tests/test_extensions_api.py backend/tests/test_catalog_api.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_extension_registry.py tests/test_extension_doctor.py tests/test_extensions_api.py tests/test_catalog_api.py
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
refactor: extract load_pretrain_weights + apply_lora into models/weights.py (#840)

Eliminates duplicated weight-loading and LoRA application logic between
detr.py (_load_pretrain_weights_into, _apply_lora_to) and module_model.py
(_load_pretrain_weights, _apply_lora).  The canonical unified implementation
lives in the new src/rfdetr/models/weights.py and both callers delegate to it.

Key changes:
- New src/rfdetr/models/weights.py: load_pretrain_weights() uses the more
  complete module_model.py logic (Pydantic model_fields_set for user-override
  detection, auto-align to fine-tuned checkpoints, expand-back for explicit
  num_classes) plus class_names extraction from detr.py.  apply_lora() is
  the unified LoRA application function.
- detr.py: removes ~80 lines of private weight/LoRA methods; delegates to
  weights.py; fixes mutable COCO_CLASS_NAMES returned directly from
  class_names property (now returns list copy).
- module_model.py: removes ~95 lines of private weight/LoRA methods;
  delegates to weights.py.
- tests/models/test_weights.py: 8 new characterization tests (4 reinit
  scenarios, 2 class_names extraction, 2 LoRA application).
- test_load_pretrain_weights.py, test_module_model.py, test_detr_shim.py:
  migrated patch targets and call sites to rfdetr.models.weights.
- Pin torchvision>=0.17.0 (aligns with torch>=2.2.0 floor)
- Pin pydantic>=2.0,<3 (formalises existing v2-only API usage)
- Move peft from core deps to [lora] and [train] optional extras
- Replace top-level `from peft import PeftModel` in backbone.py with
  lazy try/except import inside Backbone.export()
- Fix merge_and_unload() result not being assigned back to self.encoder
- Add ImportError hints in _apply_lora_to() and _apply_lora() pointing
  users to `pip install rfdetr[lora]`
- Add characterization test: importing backbone must not pull peft into
  sys.modules
- Add regression test: Backbone.export() replaces encoder with merged model
- `_RemovedModuleFinder.find_spec` now short-circuits on any `fullname`
  that does not start with `rfdetr.`, preventing bare top-level imports
  such as `import util` or `import deploy` from being hijacked by the
  RF-DETR migration finder after `import rfdetr`.
- Replace the type-identity dedup guard with a `sys`-level sentinel
  (`sys._rfdetr_removed_finder`) so `importlib.reload(rfdetr)` never
  inserts a second finder into `sys.meta_path`.
- Add two characterization tests: scope guard (bare names return None)
  and reload idempotency (finder count unchanged after reload).

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: pre-commit-ci[bot] <66853113+pre-commit-ci[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): only fail Semgrep job when actual findings exist (#812)

* chore: trigger CI

* fix(well-known): add credential sharing for admin app and remove www from intent filters

Add delegate_permission/common.get_login_creds to admin app's assetlinks.json
so Google Play Console can enable credential sharing for usebaci.com.

Remove www.usebaci.com from admin app's iOS associatedDomains and Android
intentFilters since Vercel 308-redirects www → bare domain, which causes
Android/Apple verification to fail.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve type errors in slash-command, imei-check, and about.md test

- Add explicit CommandProps type to novel slash-command callbacks
- Remove invalid export from IMEI_SERVICE_TIERS constant (route files
  can only export route handlers)
- Add about_page field to about.md route test mock data
- Add slash-command.test.tsx with novel module mock

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(types): replace any casts with Tiptap module augmentation in slash-command

Add declare module entries for taskList, bulletList, and orderedList
commands alongside existing productExtension augmentation. Removes all
4 biome-ignore/any casts from slash-command.tsx.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): only fail Semgrep job when actual findings exist
